claims.text: Fabricio voted for CS/CS/SB 1718 (2023), Florida's comprehensive immigration-enforcement law. The statute prohibits local governments from adopting sanctuary policies that restrict law enforcement cooperation with federal immigration authorities, mandates E-Verify for private employers with 25 or more employees, requires Florida law enforcement agencies to honor ICE detainers, and bars local funds from being used to issue identification to individuals without lawful immigration status. The bill was signed by Governor DeSantis on May 10, 2023, and took effect July 1, 2023. As a Cuban-American Republican representing Hialeah — a community with strong anti-communist, pro-rule-of-law values — Fabricio's support for border enforcement and anti-sanctuary legislation reflects his district's core concerns.

Fabricio serves as Vice Chair of the Florida House Ways & Means Committee and his campaign materials document that since 2020 he has 'played a vital role in sponsoring and supporting numerous bills that have lowered taxes, improved our economy, and protected law enforcement officers from the agenda of the radical left.' His fiscal record as a small-business owner (managing partner of The Fabricio Law Firm and owner of True Freedom Title, LLC) and his committee role reflect a pro-limited-government, anti-deficit posture consistent with the rubric's economic-stewardship standard.

claims.text: Florida Right to Life endorsed Omar Blanco in the November 2024 general election for HD-115. He stated on his iVoterGuide questionnaire: 'Human life begins at conception and deserves legal protection at every stage until natural death.' He also endorsed the Born Alive Abortion Survivors Protection Act and opposed all taxpayer funding for abortion providers including Planned Parenthood. Blanco, the son of Cuban exiles, represents parts of Miami, Hialeah, and Doral in Miami-Dade County.

The Christian Family Coalition Florida — a leading pro-family, pro-religious-liberty advocacy group — named Blanco among honored GOP lawmakers at their 2025 Legislative Victory Breakfast at Trump National Doral (August 2025), citing his support for CFC-backed legislation during the 2025 session including expanded parental rights measures and HB 1205 (election integrity reform). Blanco campaigned explicitly on 'further protect[ing] parental rights in education' and stated on iVoterGuide: 'Parents should have the right to make decisions for their minor children.'

Supported HB 1205 (2025), Florida's citizen initiative petition reform bill, described by the Republican Party of Florida as an 'election integrity' measure and celebrated by the Christian Family Coalition as a 2025 session victory. The bill reformed Florida's constitutional initiative amendment process. The RPOF described the bill as protecting election integrity by tightening rules on petition-driven ballot initiatives. Blanco's recognition by CFC for 2025 session votes confirms his support for the legislation.

claims.text: Florida Right to Life endorsed Mike Redondo in the November 2024 general election for HD-118. Redondo, a Catholic personal injury attorney and son of Cuban exiles, represents west Miami-Dade County (near Zoo Miami / Kendall). He first won his seat via special election on December 5, 2023, and was re-elected in November 2024 with 68.3% of the vote. He was subsequently elected Speaker-designate for the 2030 Florida House term by unanimous vote of all 22 GOP freshmen, making him a key future figure in Florida Republican leadership.

Sponsored HB 757 (2025), signed into law as Chapter 2025-99 (effective October 1, 2025), making it a felony to create, possess, or distribute AI-generated or deepfake non-consensual sexual images — including of minors (3rd-degree felony for soliciting or viewing; 2nd-degree for intent to distribute). The bill passed the Florida Legislature and was signed by Governor DeSantis, establishing criminal penalties for digital sexual exploitation that protects children and women from AI-generated abuse.

Co-sponsored HB 1505 (2025), expanding parental consent requirements for minors' healthcare — including STI treatment, surveys, biofeedback, and DNA records — and strengthening parents' right to be involved in their minor children's medical decisions. Redondo's campaign platform states he stands with 'President Trump and the America First movement' and lists parental involvement and constitutional defense as core priorities. He self-identifies as Catholic and describes his Cuban exile family background as central to his conviction that government must not supplant parental and family authority.

profile.background: Rory Diamond — Jacksonville City Council District 13 (Atlantic Beach, Jacksonville Beach, Neptune Beach, Mayport, Intracoastal West). Elected May 2019; re-elected 2023 (defeated write-in only). Former litigator at international firms; former Special Assistant U.S. Attorney. Captain, U.S. Army / Florida National Guard JAG — deployed to the Middle East with the 40th Infantry Division in 2023 as Chief of Military Justice; returned to council duty Feb 2024. Former CEO (2014-2023) of K9s For Warriors (national veterans / service-dog nonprofit headquartered near Jacksonville); co-authored the federal PAWS Act (Puppies Assisting Wounded Servicemembers) signed in 2021 with then-Rep. Ron DeSantis. Wife reportedly Lauren Blocker. Term-limited from D13 after 2027; widely reported considering 2027 Jacksonville mayoral run.

profile.background: Terrance Freeman — Jacksonville City Council At-Large Group 1, R; age ~51. Career: Executive Council Assistant to former Council Member Aaron Bowman starting 2015; subsequently Senior Project Manager / Senior Program Manager of Apprenticeship Programs at Miller Electric Company. Appointed by Gov. Rick Scott to Council District 10 in 2018; elected At-Large Group 1 in 2019; re-elected 2023 with 65% (100,297 votes — highest of any candidate on ballot). Council Vice President 2021; Council President 2022-2023. Chairs Rules Committee; serves on Finance Committee. Married, father of five. Bachelor's and master's degrees in physical education / sports psychology. Father was an Army veteran and Baptist minister; mother a career social worker. Submitted resignation May 28, 2026 to run for Florida House District 12; formally vacates council seat Nov 2, 2026; HD-12 Republican primary Aug 18, 2026 (currently unopposed); special election to fill council seat Aug 18, 2026.

profile.background: John Hugh 'Buddy' Dyer (b. Aug 7, 1958, Kissimmee FL). BA civil engineering Brown University; JD University of Florida College of Law. Civil engineer / attorney by training. FL State Senate 1992-2002 (Senate Minority Leader 1998-2000); 2002 Democratic nominee for FL Attorney General (lost to Charlie Crist). Mayor of Orlando since March 2003 — 48th and longest-serving mayor. Divorced Karen Caudill 2017 (2 sons); remarried Susie Pinson Galloway Shields 2021. 2005 absentee-ballot fraud charge dismissed; reinstated by Gov. Bush. Central public figure in 2016 Pulse nightclub aftermath.

profile.background: Charles Thomas 'Tom' Keen III. Orlando City Commissioner, District 1 (officially nonpartisan ballot); personal voter registration Democrat (previously Republican, party-switcher). Sworn in January 12, 2026 after defeating incumbent Jim Gray 53.97%-31.25% in November 4, 2025 election. Prior offices: Florida House District 35 (Jan 27, 2024 – Nov 5, 2024; lost rematch to Erika Booth 48.02%-51.98%); 2017 Orlando D1 race (lost to Gray); 2022 FL House D35 Democratic primary (lost by 57 votes); 2012 IN State Senate D16 (lost). Bachelor's degree, University of Cincinnati. U.S. Navy retired Commander (O-5); Naval Flight Officer designated 1983; airborne reconnaissance with shore tour at U.S. Embassy London. Post-military: manager at Collins Aerospace. Previously served on Orlando Citizens' Police Review Board and Mayor's Veterans Advisory Council. Current 4-year term: Jan 12, 2026 – Jan 2030.

profile.background: Antonio 'Tony' Ortiz. Orlando City Commissioner, District 2 (east Orlando / Azalea Park / Curry Ford / Lee Vista), since May 1, 2008; re-elected unopposed 2023; current term ends January 10, 2028. Officially nonpartisan; personal voter registration switched from Republican to Democrat on May 6, 2025 (cited GOP positions on LGBTQ rights, voting rights, immigration, climate); reportedly considering a 2027 Orlando mayoral run. Born Manatí, Puerto Rico; Orlando resident since 1989 — first/only Puerto Rican commissioner of City of Orlando. U.S. Marine Corps 1984, 9 years active+reserve, Persian Gulf War veteran. 14 years Orlando Police Department (Special Task Forces, Gangs Unit, Uniform Drug Unit, SWAT). Accounting/banking professional. Past President, Florida League of Cities. University of Central Florida.

profile.background: Patty Sheehan, b. ~1962. BA in art from University of Central Florida; prior career as administrator at Florida Dept. of Agriculture and Consumer Services. Elected 2000 — first openly gay elected official in Central Florida. Has spoken publicly about surviving an Orlando-based 'ex-gay' conversion ministry (Eleutheros) at age 18 — defining personal narrative that informs her LGBTQ advocacy. Drove Orlando's Domestic Partner Registry and LGBTQ employment nondiscrimination ordinance. Central public figure in 2016 Pulse nightclub aftermath (the club sits in her district); co-led the OPD Safe Place Initiative with then-Chief John Mina. Hosted a drag queen (Darcel Stevens) at her 2023 victory party in open defiance of Florida's drag-restriction law. HRC / Equality Florida / Victory Fund-aligned throughout 25+ year tenure.

candidates.notes: Orlando City Commissioner D5 (NP ballot, actual Democrat); sworn June 24, 2024 (special), re-elected Nov 4, 2025; term to 2029. [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] HARD evidence (mostly from 2022 HD41 Winter Garden Vox candidate questionnaire + 2024-25 council platform): (a) 2022 questionnaire — 'women should have access to reproductive healthcare and not be forced to give birth to children that might create a financial hardship for them' — sanctity_of_life q1 FALSE; (b) self-described 'survivor of gun violence' (lost husband); platform supports 'reduction of gun violence' and school-safety measures including metal detectors / standardized LE training / secure access points — self_defense q1 FALSE (gun-control orientation; FL 790.33 preempts city ordinances regardless); (c) Fraternal Order of Police endorsement; platform centers 'community policing'; took credit for '27% drop in crime' under her tenure — public_justice q0 TRUE (FOP endorsement + affirmative public backing clears high bar). NULL on biblical marriage, family/school (no city school authority), Christian liberty, economic stewardship (no individual Rose vote against the 12-year flat millage on record either way), election integrity, refuse_state_overreach, and border (Mayor Dyer not Rose handled the Jan 2026 ICE-facility statement). Religion not publicly stated. Active investigation flag (not rubric-scored): March 2026 OPD referred her for FDLE investigation over alleged use of anonymous private email accounts from City Hall network to send harassing messages to a Pulse survivor advocacy group; Regina Hill has also filed a Nov 2025 election-fraud lawsuit against Rose and the canvassing board (Rose called it 'frivolous'). Both pending; flagged as governance-conduct context only.

profile.background: Shaniqua 'Shan' Rose. Orlando City Commissioner, District 5, since June 24, 2024 (took office via special runoff after Gov. DeSantis suspended prior Commissioner Regina Hill in April 2024 over elder-abuse/fraud allegations); re-elected Nov 4, 2025 defeating Hill ~52%-33% with Lawanna Gelser ~15%; current 4-year term runs to 2029. Officially nonpartisan ballot; actual party affiliation Democrat (filed 2022 Democratic primary for FL House District 41; FL Dem Party chair Nikki Fried publicly celebrated her 2025 win; endorsed by Run For Something). Born to a St. Lucian immigrant family, raised in Brooklyn NY; relocated to Central Florida 2000. Education: Poinciana HS; AA General Studies, Valencia College (2007); BA Supervision & Management, Polk State College (2015); MA Public Administration, American Public University (2018). Career: former City of Orlando staff in Economic Development (Downtown Development Board/CRA) and Permitting Office; founder/CEO of Change For The Community (CFTC, est. July 4, 2019); Executive Director of Eatonville CRA. Civic: NAACP, League of Women Voters, American Red Cross, National Congress of Black Women. Single mother; widow (lost husband to gun violence).

Verified via the official City of Orlando roster page (orlando.gov/Our-Government/Mayor-City-Council/) which lists Burns at D6 and contains no entry for Ings on the current council.

profile.background: Samuel B. Ings — FORMER Orlando City Commissioner, District 6 (West Orlando — Pine Hills, parts of Parramore / Mercy Drive corridor). Elected 2006, re-elected 2010 and 2014. Did NOT seek re-election in 2018; ran for Mayor of Orlando in November 2018 (lost to incumbent Buddy Dyer; finished second behind Aretha Simons). Has not held elected office since. Bakari F. Burns has held Orlando D6 continuously since the 2018 election. Background: ~25+ years sworn officer with the Orlando Police Department, retired as sergeant before entering elected office. Small-business owner (private security / consulting) post-OPD. Officially nonpartisan ballot; personal voter registration Democrat. Publicly identifies as Christian; honored by predominantly Black Protestant churches in west Orlando across his career.

He then voted with the 6-1 FL Supreme Court majority in Planned Parenthood v. State of Florida (April 2024) upholding the 6-week heartbeat ban, demonstrating a lifelong commitment to protecting the unborn.

As a member of the House Judiciary Committee, Canady cosponsored the Defense of Marriage Act (DOMA, 1996) and submitted the committee report (H.Rept. 104-664) recommending its passage — defining federal marriage as one man and one woman. DOMA passed the House 342-67 on July 12, 1996. Canady's committee leadership was central to enacting DOMA into law.

profile.background: 127th Mayor of Tallahassee, Democrat. Born Dec 7, 1972 in Miami. Served three terms on Leon County Commission District 3 (2006-2018), chairing 2010-11 and 2016-17. Elected Mayor Nov 6, 2018 (succeeded Andrew Gillum); re-elected 2022. Announced Aug 11, 2025 he will not seek a third term; 2026 mayoral race is open-seat. Wife Virginia; two sons. Father (J. Scott Dailey) was a Leon County School Board member. Sigma Chi at FSU.

profile.religion: Christian — 40-year member of Bethel Missionary Baptist Church, Tallahassee; serves on several ministries (per City of Tallahassee official bio and FAMUAN feature).
profile.background: Dianne Williams-Cox (b. Quincy, FL). James A. Shanks HS honors graduate 1981; BS Data Processing Technology (FAMU); MBA (Nova Southeastern). Career: software developer GM; Harris Government Systems; 19 years State of Florida; FAMU adjunct professor. Currently Direct Services Quality Assurance Manager for Voices for Florida and owner of DWC Management Consulting Services. Married 33+ years to Thomas 'Tommy' Cox; three adult sons, six grandchildren. Founder, Southside United Citizens Action Alliance; former Tallahassee-Leon County Planning Commission; ran for FL House District 8 in 2014. 2018 Capital Outlook 'Community Activist of the Year.' First elected to Tallahassee City Commission Nov 2018; re-elected outright Aug 2022. Past Vice Chair Leon County DEC; past President Capital City Democratic Women's Club; Region 1 Chair Democratic Women of Florida. 40-year member of Bethel Missionary Baptist Church Tallahassee, multiple ministries.

candidates.name: Curtis Richardson
candidates.office: City Commissioner, Seat 2, City of Tallahassee, Florida (first elected 2014; re-elected 2018; re-elected Nov 2024 in runoff defeating Dot Inman-Johnson; term to Nov 2028; currently serving as Mayor Pro Tem)
candidates.notes: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Web-verified sitting Seat 2 City Commissioner and current Mayor Pro Tem (talgov.com bio + WFSU Nov 2024 re-election coverage + bethel1865.org board roster). Sweep of talgov.com, bethel1865.org, Tallahassee Reports, WCTV, WTXL, WFSU, Tallahassee Democrat, Panhandle Press, LiveAction, Florida Politics, Ballotpedia. HARD evidence — 3 TRUE / 4 FALSE / 23 NULL after exhaustive sweep. TRUE: christian_liberty.0 (Bethel AME Church Tallahassee usher AND trustee — explicit public Christian profession on both his city bio and the church's official board roster). public_justice.0 (sustained affirmative pro-LE advocacy clears the high-bar: led FY2024 millage hike specifically to fund 20 ADDITIONAL TPD officers and TPD technology; voted YES on $625K federal grant to hire police officers Nov 2025; publicly defended pro-police budget at conservative candidate forum — 'We raised the millage rate in order to pay for police services' — not just a budget vote, an explicit pro-LE program). border_immigration.2 (Oct 22, 2025 voted AGAINST motion to rescind Tallahassee's 287(g) agreement with ICE — agreement REMAINS in force; TPD cooperates with ICE; vote stands even though rhetoric framed reluctant compliance with state-removal threats). FALSE: sanctity_of_life.0 (explicit pro-choice on record: 'I believe in a woman's right to choose' and 'that is a choice between my wife and me'; 2021 NO vote on the city's pro-choice resolution was procedural disbelief that the Legislature would listen, NOT a pro-life vote — LiveAction + WCTV). family_child_sovereignty.3 (self-identified as 'one of the biggest opponents' of Jeb Bush's opportunity scholarship program — opposes FL school choice / voucher expansion). economic_stewardship.2 (voted YES on FY2025 budget 3-2 at 4.42 mills above rollback AND on FY2026 budget at 4.4072 mills; explicitly rejected the rollback proposal that would have required ~$3.9M of cuts — 'we raised the millage rate in order to pay for police services' is honest framing, but it scores FALSE on the fiscal-restraint cell as written). refuse_state_overreach.0 (re: 287(g) said 'It would not be in my best interest to put myself in a position to potentially be removed from office' — explicit compliance posture on a conservative-rubric-coded state mandate). NULL HOLDS: biblical_marriage NULL — Tallahassee historically issued Pride proclamations under his tenure but no specific Richardson floor vote was sourced this batch; the 2026 unanimous review-of-DEI vote was compliance with state law, not affirmative marriage defense; honest hold. self_defense NULL — no sourced 2A statement post-FSU April 2025 shooting located. All other cells NULL — no cited evidence on sanctity_of_life q1-4, biblical_marriage q0-4, family q0-2,4, christian_liberty q1-4, economic_stewardship q0/1/3/4, election_integrity (any), public_justice q1-4, refuse_state_overreach q1-4, border_immigration q0/1/3/4, self_defense (any).

profile.religion: Christian — long-time member of Bethel AME Church Tallahassee, serving as usher and trustee (publicly listed on bethel1865.org Board of Trustees roster and confirmed on talgov.com bio).
profile.background: Curtis B. Richardson (b. Oct 8, 1956, Jacksonville, FL; raised Green Cove Springs). BS Psychology (FSU); MA Counseling Psychology (UWF); MS School Psychology (FSU 1983). School psychologist Gadsden County 1983-1991; staff to FL Education Commissioner Betty Castor; Deputy Director of Cabinet Affairs under Gov. Lawton Chiles; Director of School Improvement Gadsden. Leon County School Board 1990-1996 (first Black male elected, served as Chair). Florida House of Representatives District 8 (D, Tallahassee) 4 terms 2000-2008. Tallahassee City Commission Seat 2 since 2014 (re-elected 2018, re-elected Nov 2024 over Dot Inman-Johnson). Currently Mayor Pro Tem. Married to Leon County Judge Nina Ashenafi-Richardson. Long-time member of Bethel AME Tallahassee (usher / trustee).

candidates.notes: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: Seat 3, sworn Nov 19, 2018; term ends Nov 16, 2026; NOT seeking re-election to Seat 3 — filed Aug 11, 2025 to run for Tallahassee Mayor in the Aug 18, 2026 primary (Seat 3 will be open). Leading progressive bloc voice (with Porter); endorsed Bernie Sanders 2020. HARD evidence: PERSONALLY MOVED the Oct 2021 abortion-access resolution (passed 3-2; Matlow + Porter + Dailey YES); after Dobbs said the work 'is more important than ever' — sanctity_of_life q0 FALSE (Florida Politics, WCTV, Tallahassee Reports). Equality Florida 'Friend' status 2026 Tallahassee Gala; aligned with Capital Equality Democratic Caucus — biblical_marriage q2/q4 FALSE (Equality FL). May 2026 publicly defended Pride Festival visibility against SB 1134 ('outright cruel,' attacked law for blocking posts about 'Pride Festival') — biblical_marriage q4 FALSE; family_child_sovereignty q2 FALSE (WCTV DEI coverage). MADE motion to RESCIND Tallahassee's 287(g) ICE agreement Oct 22, 2025 (failed 2-3, Matlow + Porter YES; Dailey/Richardson/Williams-Cox NO) — border_immigration q0/q1/q2 all FALSE (WTXL, WFSU). Said he was 'disgusted' by ICE raids. PRE-MOTION led Aug 20, 2025 procedural vote to direct city attorney to explore challenging 287(g) in court. Feb 2023 publicly called FL constitutional-carry legislation 'one of the most reckless proposals' — self_defense q0 FALSE. Voted against multiple TPD-funding budget cycles; PAC (One Tallahassee) amplified video described as 'anti-law enforcement' promoting Inman-Johnson — public_justice q0/q3 FALSE (Tallahassee Reports). DEVIATION: Consistent NO on millage above rollback (2023 8.5% hike, 2024 4.42 millage, 2025 forced down from 4.42 to 4.4072) — econ q2 TRUE on paper, but explicitly motivated by anti-fire-fee / anti-establishment progressive populism, not Reagan fiscal conservatism. candidates.name: Kenneth T. Welch
candidates.notes: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: 54th Mayor of St. Petersburg, sworn Jan 6, 2022; declared candidate in Aug 18, 2026 primary (Wikipedia 2026 mayoral race page). March 2026 polling showed Welch (19%) trailing Charlie Crist (23%) in a 7-way race incl. Brandi Gabbard, Jim Large (R), Maria Scruggs, Kevin Batdorf, Paul Congemi. HIGH-VALUE DEVIATION: Welch openly identifies with his family's COGIC heritage and publicly cites 'pray, plan, prepare, perform' as his guiding principles (prayer first) — christian_liberty q0 TRUE (Tampa Magazines long-form interview). HARD evidence on LGBTQ+ cells: personally raises the Pride flag over City Hall every year (12th annual 2025); 2022 proclamation: 'We cannot and will not stand silent while our fundamental rights of freedom, choice and self-determination are stripped by anyone … including politicians in Washington and Tallahassee'; 2026 wrapped City Hall steps in Pride colors and pledged ~$300K-equivalent in-kind public-safety support to St. Pete Pride despite SB 1134 (St. Pete Catalyst, Florida Politics, CL Tampa) — biblical_marriage q0/q2/q4 FALSE. AFFIRMATIVE policing posture: $171M SPPD budget, body cams/tasers/drones, public partnership with Chief Holloway, publicly thanked Rep. Luna for $480K in federal cruiser funding ('This kind of support means a lot … setting a national standard') — public_justice q0 TRUE (St. Pete Catalyst, St Pete Rising). HIGH-VALUE DEVIATION: maintains SPPD's 287(g) ICE partnership and has REFUSED activist pressure to cancel it ('Mayor Ken Welch has the capacity to get rid of the 287(g) agreement, but he is actively choosing not to do so' — Florida Phoenix / WMNF); St. Pete is NOT a sanctuary city — border_immigration q2 TRUE. ECON: FY23 budget approved with ~13% effective property-tax increase (~$20.5M more revenue, St. Pete Catalyst); Welch publicly blasted Crist for proposing tax cuts — economic_stewardship q2 FALSE. ABORTION: administration raised 'process and precedent' concerns about the Tampa Bay Abortion Fund grant proposal (council killed 6-2); no affirmative pro-life position either — sanctity_of_life cells null. STATE PUSHBACK: led FL mayors against SB 1134 anti-DEI bill and defied it on Pride; this is local-control posture but directionally leftward (defending DEI/Pride), so refuse_state_overreach cells null under the conservative-rubric reading. NO direct evidence on schools (mayor lane), election administration, christian_liberty q2-q4, public_justice q1-q4, or self-defense (FL preemption blocks municipal gun regs) — those cells null.

profile.religion: Pentecostal Church of God in Christ (COGIC) family heritage — uncle Clarence pastored Prayer Tower COGIC in the Gas Plant District; father David sang in the choir and taught Sunday school there. Publicly cites 'pray, plan, prepare, perform' as his four guiding principles (prayer listed first).
profile.education: MBA, Florida A&M University; BA Accounting, University of South Florida
profile.background: 54th Mayor of St. Petersburg, first elected Nov 2021 (St. Pete's first Black mayor), sworn Jan 6, 2022; running for re-election in the Aug 18, 2026 primary (Nov 3, 2026 runoff if needed). Prior: Pinellas County Commissioner 2000-2022 (District 7). Accountant at Florida Power Corporation. Third-generation St. Pete native (b. Aug 15, 1964). Wife Donna, two daughters.

candidates.name: Copley Gerdes
candidates.notes: St Petersburg City Council D1 since Jan 6, 2022; re-elected UNOPPOSED Nov 5, 2024; current term to Jan 4, 2029; Council CHAIR since Dec 2024 (also Budget/Finance/Taxation Chair). Officially NP ballot. [2026-06 RESOLUTE LOCAL refinement] HARD evidence: (a) biblical_marriage q4 FALSE — as Council Chair, PERSONALLY INTRODUCED the June 4, 2026 Pride flag-raising ceremony at City Hall (Tampa Bay Times / Watermark Out News); in October 2025 voted YES to advance Councilmember Driscoll's 'Harmony' (LGBTQ+) and 'History' (Black history) city-flag variations (joining Driscoll, Givens, Figgs-Sanders, Floyd, Hanewicz; only Harting voted no). His own quote: 'I want to throw a haymaker… I don't want to throw a jab.' (b) economic_stewardship q2 FALSE — as Budget/Finance/Taxation Chair voted YES on the FY24 city budget that Catalyst headlined as 'begrudgingly approves budget, 13% tax increase'; YES on the $287.5M Rays stadium bonds + $142M Gas Plant District bonds (4-3, July 2024); YES on the original 5-3 stadium agreement. Pattern: chairs the budget committee, votes for major debt issuance + tax increases. NULL on sanctity_of_life (no St Pete pro-life resolution; no Gerdes statement on abortion), family_child_sovereignty (Pinellas school board is separate authority), christian_liberty (Catholic faith documented in profile.religion but no policy vote — religion is biographical not scoreable on its own per evidence-first rule), election_integrity (no Gerdes-specific election-admin vote), public_justice (CALL — Community Assistance & Life Liaison — is a partial-diversion social-worker-first model that diverts certain 911 calls FROM sworn officers; Gerdes praised CALL participation; no affirmative pro-police statement clears the rubric's high bar), refuse_state_overreach (his pro-LGBTQ-flag stance opposing DeSantis state actions defends progressive priorities — not the rubric's conservative-subsidiarity anchor; held null), border_immigration (the 2017 'sanctuary city' comments were from his FATHER Charlie Gerdes, NOT Copley — do not attribute), and self_defense (FL 790.33 preempts). DISCIPLINE FLAGS: father/son distinction matters (Charlie Gerdes was the 2017 sanctuary-city commenter); Catholic-school + Catholic-university religion degree is biographically suggestive but NOT scored as a policy verdict per evidence-first rule.

profile.religion: Roman Catholic (St. Petersburg Catholic High School alumnus; B.A. in Religion with Catholic Studies emphasis from Saint Leo University, a Catholic institution; personal faith is documented but no specific policy position derived from it)
profile.background: Charles 'Copley' Gerdes. City of St. Petersburg, Florida City Council District 1 since January 6, 2022 (special election to replace Robert Blackmon); re-elected UNOPPOSED Nov 5, 2024; current term ends January 4, 2029. Officially NONPARTISAN ballot. Council CHAIR since December 2024 (elected after five rounds of voting); also Chair of Budget, Finance & Taxation Committee. Listed #21 on Florida Politics' Tampa Bay Most Powerful Politicians. Family: son of former St. Pete Councilmember Charlie Gerdes (also held D1) and nephew of city administrator Rob Gerdes. Education: St. Petersburg Catholic HS; B.A. Religion (Catholic Studies emphasis), Saint Leo University. Profession: financial advisor with Northwestern Mutual since 2011; also Director of Special Projects / Strategic Development at family law firm Keane, Reese, Vesely & Gerdes P.A. Married Cecily Valenty (2016); two children. Catholic faith publicly documented via education path but no derived policy position scored.

[2026-06 RESOLUTE LOCAL refinement] HARD evidence: (a) sanctity_of_life q1 FALSE — Feb 23, 2023 Council Chair Gabbard initially SUPPORTED a $50,000 city allocation to the Tampa Bay Abortion Fund; on April 6, 2023 reversed and voted against it but explicitly cited political-risk calculus (state-Republican retaliation threats, Disney/Andrew Warren examples), NOT pro-life conviction. Initial pro-fund support is the dispositive position. (b) biblical_marriage q4 FALSE — attended/participated in June 4, 2026 Pride flag-raising at City Hall during 'Shine with Pride 2026'; posted official 'Happy Pride St Pete' on D2 Facebook; as mayoral candidate (April 2026) condemned FL anti-DEI law SB 1134 ('every resident — regardless of their race or whom they love — has an equal seat at the table'). (c) economic_stewardship q2 FALSE — voted YES on the $6.5B Rays Stadium / Gas Plant District deal July 18, 2024 (5-3) AND on the Dec 5, 2024 bond authorization (long-term city debt for a private-team stadium). Quote: 'We need to move this forward because, quite frankly, I think we're calling their bluff by doing so.' Also supports general-obligation infrastructure bonds (future-generation debt justified by resilience needs). NULL on family/school (Pinellas school board is separate authority), christian_liberty (no public stance; religion not stated), election_integrity (Pinellas SoE handles elections), public_justice (no affirmative pro-police statement located; mayoral 'public safety' platform pillar lacks specifics), refuse_state_overreach (her anti-DEI/anti-preemption stance defends progressive priorities — not the rubric's conservative-subsidiarity anchor; held null), border_immigration (no Gabbard-specific ICE/sanctuary statement located; she joined council Jan 2018 after the 2017 sanctuary-city statement under Mayor Kriseman), and self_defense (FL 790.33 preempts).

profile.background: Brandi Gabbard. City of St. Petersburg, Florida City Council District 2 (NE St. Pete — Barcley Estates / Northeast) since January 2018; re-elected unopposed 2021; current term ends January 7, 2027 (TERM-LIMITED). Officially NONPARTISAN ballot; personal voter registration Democrat. Filed March 2026 to run for St. Petersburg MAYOR in November 2026, challenging incumbent Mayor Ken Welch — positioning to the LEFT of Welch on DEI/LGBTQ issues. Past Council Chair. candidates.name: Mike Harting
candidates.office: City Council, District 3, City of St. Petersburg, Florida (elected Nov 5, 2024 runoff over Pete Boland with 50.38%; sworn in Jan 2, 2025; term to Jan 4, 2029; succeeded term-limited Republican Ed Montanari)
candidates.notes: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Web-verified sitting District 3 Councilmember (stpete.org D3 bio + Florida Politics Nov 2024 runoff coverage + Bay News 9 swearing-in). Sweep of stpete.org, mike4stpete.com, Florida Politics, Ballotpedia, Bay News 9, WUSF, St. Pete Catalyst, Tampa Bay Business & Wealth, San Pedro Gazette. HARD evidence — 1 TRUE / 1 FALSE / 28 NULL after exhaustive sweep. candidates.office: Mayor of Hialeah, Florida (former · resigned April 27, 2025 to join The Southern Group, a D.C. lobbying firm)
candidates.notes: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] CURRENCY FIX: Bovo is NOT the sitting Mayor of Hialeah — he resigned April 27, 2025 to join The Southern Group, a D.C. lobbying firm. Status moved to former; candidacy_status not_running. Interim mayor Jacqueline Garcia-Roves now holds the seat. HARD evidence (cited) on retained positions: (a) biblical_marriage q2 TRUE — as Miami-Dade Commissioner Dec 2, 2014, Bovo voted NO on the transgender-inclusive Human Rights Ordinance (passed 8-3), publicly saying 'There are cases of the deviants who will take advantage of this' and conditioning support on a bathroom-use carve-out; direct rejection of gender-identity-as-protected-class framing. (b) biblical_marriage q4 FALSE — Bovo CO-SPONSORED the Miami-Dade LGBTQ Advisory Board ordinance in 2019, publicly thanked by Equality Florida; that is direct affirmative promotion of LGBTQ+ public policy. The record is genuinely mixed (q2 anti / q4 pro) and is scored that way per the no-overread, no-inference rule. (c) public_justice q0 TRUE — 2020 mayoral campaign explicitly opposed defunding/restructuring police: 'Rethinking everything about our police only feeds into what I think is a very, very loud, minority'; police-union support based on no-defund record; affirmative anti-defund advocacy clearing the FXBG high bar. (d) border_immigration q2 TRUE — as Hialeah Mayor pushed Resolution 2025-073 (March 2025) authorizing 287(g) MOA with ICE, explicitly framed as avoiding sanctuary-city designation under Tallahassee pressure. NOT scored on border_immigration q1 (mandatory deportation): Bovo explicitly HEDGED — 'If the question is, are we gonna pull people and ask for their immigration papers? That's not what we're trained to do, that's not what we're authorized to do' — runs against the q1 high bar. NOT scored on refuse_state_overreach despite 2020-21 anti-mask-mandate statements ('I'm not in favor of mandates on anything'): he was ALIGNED with DeSantis, not refusing state directives — DeSantis was also anti-mandate, so this isn't 'local resistance to state overreach' on the rubric reading. NOT scored on christian_liberty q3 despite routine Hialeah council invocation practice: invocations are institutional, not Bovo-led action. NO EVIDENCE FOUND on sanctity of life (the Hialeah pro-life-clinic-vandalism 'hate crime' framing is sympathetic but case-specific, not a personhood/abolition statement), school-choice / parental rights, election integrity, or self-defense — those cells null. profile.religion: Roman Catholic (parishioner; specific parish not extractable from the official Hialeah bio PDF — binary not OCR-readable in this sweep)
profile.education: Miami Dade College (AA), Florida International University (BA)
profile.background: Esteban 'Steve' Bovo Jr., b. June 12, 1962 in Queens, NY to Esteban Bovo Carás (2506 Brigade / Bay of Pigs veteran). AA Business Administration, Miami-Dade Community College (1983); BS Political Science, Florida International University (1987). Pre-politics: marketing/advertising executive, Hialeah Park Race Track. Career: Hialeah City Council 1998-2008; Florida House District 110 (2008-2011, resigned to run for county); Miami-Dade County Commissioner District 13 (2011-2020, served as Chair); ran for Miami-Dade Mayor 2020 (won R primary, lost general); elected Mayor of Hialeah Nov 2, 2021; RESIGNED April 27, 2025 to join The Southern Group lobbying firm in D.C., following wife Viviana Bovo (longtime Marco Rubio staffer, now State Department). Five children: Oscar, Bianca, Esteban, Sofia, Alessandro. Interim Mayor Jacqueline Garcia-Roves (City Council President) sworn in April 2025 — Hialeah's first female mayor; special election Nov 2025.

Religion unknown.

profile.education: Florida Atlantic University (BA), University of South Florida (MA)
profile.background: Gregory Scott Tony (D, b. 1978 Philadelphia) — 17th Sheriff of Broward County, first Black sheriff in county history. Appointed by Gov. Ron DeSantis January 11 2019 after DeSantis suspended Scott Israel over Parkland response. Former Coral Springs PD sergeant (2005-2016), FSU football player, co-founded Blue Spear Solutions active-shooter training firm. Won 2020 D primary narrowly over Israel, re-elected November 5 2024 with 66.57% (531,594 votes) defeating independent Charles Whatley. Religion not publicly disclosed; has engaged both interfaith/Christian clergy (BOLD Justice coalition) and Islamic Center of South Florida outreach.

candidates.name: Rosie Cordero-Stutz
candidates.notes: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: Miami-Dade Sheriff (first elected in ~60 years), sworn Jan 7, 2025, term to Jan 2029 (FL Sheriffs Assn, Wikipedia, Local 10 Feb 2026 1-on-1). HARD evidence: (1) economic_stewardship q2 TRUE — publicly fought for full LEO funding, called shortfall 'defunding the police'; secured $30.8M additional after pushback; 'You can't put a price on public safety' (Local 10 July 2025). (2) public_justice q0 TRUE (HIGH BAR met) — career 28+ yr MDPD officer; publicly champions MDSO funding, staffing, anti-defund framing; endorsed by FOP, IUPA, HPOA. (3) public_justice q4 TRUE — 'My deputies will arrest anyone who has committed a crime, regardless of their immigration status' (CBS Miami) — affirmative anti-soft-on-crime. (4) border_immigration q0 TRUE — Miami-Dade signed 287(g) MOA under her, announced Feb 26-27, 2025 at Homestead with DeSantis; all 67 FL sheriffs signed; deputies undergoing 40-hr ICE training (WLRN, NBC Miami). (5) border_immigration q1 TRUE — 'If you are arrested for committing a crime and already have an immigration detainer, you will be turned over to the appropriate agency' (CBS Miami). (6) border_immigration q2 TRUE — 'We continue to support our federal partners when it comes to immigration, as we have done for many years'; joined statewide ICE partnership (WLRN). DEVIATION-FLAG: she explicitly names 'the mental health crisis' as one of three top priorities and partners with Homeless Trust + Mothers Fighting For Justice (Miami Times) — soft-on-cause public-health frame cuts against public_justice q3 → null/FALSE-leaning. NO Christian profession found; inaugural was notably Jewish-flavored — christian_liberty cells null, not TRUE. PBA split: largest state PBA endorsed her Dem opponent — only FOP/IUPA/HPOA backed her. No public 2A statements found despite Republican identification — surprising; self_defense cells null. NO EVIDENCE FOUND on sanctity_of_life, biblical_marriage, family_child_sovereignty, christian_liberty, election_integrity, refuse_state_overreach, border q3 (illegal employment), self_defense q0-q4 — those null.

profile.background: Sheriff of Miami-Dade County, Republican. First elected Miami-Dade Sheriff in ~60 years under restored FL constitutional sheriff system; sworn in Jan 7, 2025. 4-year term to Jan 2029. Won Nov 2024 with 55.7% (563,828 votes) defeating Dem James Reyes. Career: MDPD officer 1996-2024, rose to Assistant Director (2022). Born Dominican Republic ~1969-70, raised by single mother in NYC. Trump 'Complete and Total Endorsement' April 24, 2024.

candidates.name: Wayne Ivey
candidates.notes: HIGH-DEVIATION-RICH archetype-5 'Constitutional Sheriff' profile — Trump-aligned + 2A maximalist + anti-sanctuary + anti-mandate + openly Christian framing. 14 TRUE cells across PJ/RSO/BI/SD/CL/FCS clusters. HEADLINE TRUE CLUSTER: (1) RSO 5/5 TRUE — 2013 signed statement refusing federal gun-control enforcement, 'we are not the mask police' Oct 2021 deputies-will-not-enforce-school-masks, 'stands with America's workforce in fight against vaccine mandates' Oct 2021, rebuked DOJ for 'threats of violence' framing of school-board-protest parents, 'Constitutional Sheriff' public identity; (2) BI 2/2 TRUE — 'You bring that crap to Brevard County, and you're going to end up in jail' anti-sanctuary, Brevard launched 287(g) in 2019 (pre-DeSantis statewide), Feb 2025 joined DeSantis Illegal Immigration Street Task Force, BCSO awarded ~$700K immigration-enforcement funding Feb 2026; (3) SD 2/2 TRUE — viral 'arm up and prepare for an attack' video calling on citizens to arm themselves, attended April 2023 constitutional carry signing 'Criminals don't go get a permit,' Sept 2025 open-carry message announcing deputies will not enforce ban after 1st DCA ruling; (4) PJ 4/5 TRUE — Wheel of Fugitive + chain-gang program (modeled on Arpaio 2013) + 'encouraging citizens to arm themselves and confront bad guys before deputies arrive' (Marshall Project), 'zero tolerance for crime keeps FL safe' flvoicenews, June 2025 anti-riot 'we will kill you, graveyard dead' press conference with AG Uthmeier, right-wing-law-enforcement-ideology alignment; (5) CL 3/4 TRUE — Defended 'In God We Trust' patrol-car decals on Fox & Friends Nov 2019, rebuked DOJ school-board-parents-as-threats memo, spoke at Trinity Wellsprings Church Men's Ministry Breakfast March 2020 'about his faith and protecting the foundation of the nation'; (6) FCS q0 TRUE — 'We are not the mask police' framed around parents' authority over their children. SINGLE FALSE DEVIATION: PJ q4 — Gregory Edwards 2018 in-custody death (Army combat vet, restraint chair, pepper spray, spit hood, stun-gun barbs in back); BCSO fought 2+ years to release jail video, FLORIDA TODAY had to sue under Public Records Act (released Nov 2020); federal §1983 force claims dismissed Mar 2023 but state negligence claim survived — pattern of accountability resistance, not reform. SOL all NULL — sheriff lane discipline; no abortion statement located; do NOT infer from R-party or evangelical framing. BM all NULL — no specific marriage/SOGI/Pride statement located. EC q2 NULL — no documented fiscal-restraint posture in agency. CAREFUL DATA HYGIENE: research agent honestly flagged that prior batch-prompt confused (a) Marion County's Susan Lorincz/Santiago-Miranda case (Sheriff Billy Woods, NOT Brevard), (b) GA Laken Riley case (Jose Ibarra) with the Brevard Aug 2021 Paris Wilder traffic-stop ambush (deputy Brian Potters wounded NOT killed) — neither belongs to Ivey's record; not scored.

candidates.notes: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: sitting Mayor of Melbourne, FL (2nd term to Nov 2028); not on 2026 ballot. Party R (Republican Mayors Association affiliate publicly recognized 2024 re-election). HARD evidence (cited): In Sept 2022 publicly defended the Space Coast Pride permit — which included a drag queen story time component — against State Rep. Randy Fine's demand the city pull the permit, posting on Facebook that drag queen story time 'is an event that happens nationwide and is attended by both adults and children' and that the organizers 'had as much right to organize as any.' candidates.name: Rachel Sadoff
candidates.office: Clerk of the Circuit Court and Comptroller, Brevard County
candidates.notes: ARCHETYPE: career-administrator R-comptroller continuing Scott-Ellis-watchdog tradition; SINGLE HARD-TRUE on economic_stewardship q2 for the 2022 Lober purchasing-card audit. Headline finding: After unanimous BoCC request February 22 2022, Sadoff conducted full audit of Commissioner Bryan Lober (R, then-District 2)'s ~$40K p-card spending. Final audit concluded card use 'was often indiscriminate, wasteful, and not for a legitimate public use.' Over $9,000 unaccounted-for items; suspected misuse of tax-exemption certificate; personal email used to evade public-records law. Referred to FDLE for criminal investigation November 2022. When Lober personally requested she NOT audit him, Sadoff publicly disclosed the request: 'I would never let a friendship influence how myself or my office exercises authority under the law' (Space Coast Rocket, 2022). She also rejected suggestion that contracted auditors are preferable to an elected comptroller answerable to voters. Sadoff v. Board of County Commissioners of Brevard County (2022) appellate-court litigation exists per FindLaw — confirming her willingness to take BoCC to court as Comptroller. Stated mission to 'expand upon Clerk Ellis's legacy of transparency, accountability, and accessibility' — Ellis was the famously combative gadfly; Sadoff has delivered. CONSTITUTIONAL-OFFICE ARCHETYPE BREAK: unlike the other 3 Brevard constitutional officers in this batch (Blickley/Cullen/Ivey-prior who are roster_verified-only), Sadoff has produced a named-target, named-amount, public-record fiscal-watchdog action against a fellow Republican commissioner — a real on-record-deviation TRUE on economic_stewardship q2. Biblical marriage q0 honest-NULL: was deputy clerk (Viera branch manager) in 2015 when Obergefell came down, NOT a policymaker; Brevard's then-Clerk Scott Ellis quietly complied (no public refusal incident comparable to Baker Co Stacie Harvey or KY Kim Davis); no Sadoff public statement on Obergefell, same-sex marriage policy, or conscience-refusal located. Catholic-affiliation is proxy-strength (St Joseph Catholic School board + Barry MPA at Adrian-Dominican Catholic institution), not self-declared. Other categories NULL per office-scope + no findable positions.

profile.religion: Catholic (proxy: Saint Joseph Catholic School board service + Barry University MPA — not self-declared)
profile.background: Brevard County Clerk of the Circuit Court and Comptroller since January 1 2021 (sworn ad interim Jan 1 2021 after predecessor Scott Ellis resigned Dec 31 2020; officially sworn Jan 5 2021). First female Brevard Clerk in 167 years. Dual-hat role: Clerk + County Comptroller + Auditor + County Recorder. Career deputy clerk: started Brevard Clerk's office 1997 under Sandy Crawford; rose to Viera Branch Manager (Viera + Brevard County Jail). First elected 2020 unopposed after Sandra Ruizzo failed to qualify in GOP primary. Re-elected unopposed 2024 through December 31 2028. EFSC (B.A. Professional Studies); Barry University (MPA). Married to Steve Sadoff; three children. Board service: Saint Joseph Catholic School (Palm Bay), Space Coast Marine Institute youth diversion program, Bays Florida. Official bio language: family 'values public service, veterans, and law enforcement.'

profile.background: Lisa Cullen, CFC, is the elected constitutional officer Tax Collector of Brevard County, Florida (Republican). Born in Logan, West Virginia; family relocated to Brevard County early 1980s. Education: Merritt Island High School; A.A. Brevard Community College / Eastern Florida State College; B.S. Accounting & Finance, University of Central Florida. Career: began at the Brevard Tax Collector's Office front counter in 1985; Certified Florida Collector (CFC) since February 2009. First elected November 2008; assumed office January 2009; re-elected unopposed in 2012, 2016, and 2020. August 20, 2024 Republican primary: defeated County Commissioner Rita Pritchett 84.8% to 15.9%; write-in opponent Daniel Edwin Lopez withdrew early September 2024 making her re-elected unopposed in the general for a term to January 2029. President of the Florida Tax Collectors Association 2014-2015. Married to high-school sweetheart Robert Cullen, a RETIRED Brevard County Sheriff's Office Lieutenant. EFSC Foundation Board (2012-2018, Board Chair); Michael-Gene Basketball Foundation; National Homeless Veterans Support. 2016 TDC Portrait of Excellence; 2022 EFSC Distinguished Alumni. Rated 'A' by Ultra MAGA Patriots Brevard County (May 2024).

profile.background: Mariann 'Mimi' Hanley — sitting D5 council member, City of Melbourne, FL. Long-time Melbourne resident. Licensed CPA, operating Mimi Hanley CPA (700 N Wickham Rd, Melbourne); small woman-owned business owner; licensed pilot. Stated priorities: maintain/improve current services, restore the Indian River Lagoon, change codes to prohibit clear-cutting for new development, increase environmental protection. Melbourne council seats are 4-year at-large elected, officially NON-PARTISAN — but Hanley is listed by name in the 'Elected Officials' section of the Brevard County Democratic Executive Committee (brevarddems.org) and BrevardDems publicly congratulated her on Facebook, providing HARD evidence of D affiliation. Elected Nov 3, 2020 succeeding Paul Alfrey (who vacated D5 to run for Mayor); re-elected Nov 5, 2024 with ~65%. Current term runs to Nov 2028.

profile.background: Mike Jaffe — 20+ year Palm Bay resident. Director of Operations for a regional home builder; small-business owner; licensed FL realtor. Married 22 years to high-school sweetheart Kim; two children (ages 9 and 7). Little League coach. Self-described fiscal conservative focused on infrastructure (flooding / Indian River Lagoon), public safety budget protection, and responsible development. Reports knocking 5,000+ doors during 2024 campaign. Won Seat 5 with 44.24% on Nov 5, 2024 (5-way special election); sworn in + unanimously elevated to Deputy Mayor Nov 20, 2024 (Hammer nominated). Authored the Oct 17, 2025 motion to censure colleague Chandler Langevin (3-2) over Langevin's 'Deport every Indian immediately' remarks and citation of Robert Rundo, requesting Gov. DeSantis suspend Langevin under FS 112.51 + an ethics complaint. Soft-R signal: spoke at the Brevard Republican Party Straw Poll (June 22, 2024); no primary-source party registration confirmed. Announced re-election bid July 1, 2025.

profile.religion: Christian — publicly self-identified: 'committed to aligning with individuals who dedicate their talents and skills to advance God's purpose and fulfill the Great Commission'; mission 'to bring unity within the body of Christ, fostering connections that are grounded in love, service, and faith.' profile.background: Kenny Johnson, MPA — Melbourne, FL native; Bayside High School; full football scholarship to Charleston Southern University (defensive back); B.S. Criminal Justice; Master of Public Administration. Member of Omega Psi Phi Fraternity. Returned to Brevard 2013; juvenile case manager, juvenile detention officer, teacher, coach, personal trainer. Founded Top Notch Training of Brevard, Inc. (2016) and the 'Top Notch Leaders' mentoring program for 5th-6th-grade boys. First elected to Palm Bay Council Seat 4 in 2018 (~58%); re-elected 2022. Currently Deputy Mayor (rotating peer designation, sequence overlaps with Jaffe) + Chair of the Florida League of Cities Finance & Taxation Policy Committee. Established Palm Bay's Annual Black History Event, Inaugural Veterans Day Parade, Youth Advisory Board, and Civil Citation Program for misdemeanor cannabis (diversion alternative to arrest). Father; local business owner. Notably feuded with then-State Rep Randy Fine (2022 confrontation; State Attorney declined to charge Johnson — 'no prosecutable crimes').

profile.religion: Christian — explicitly professed: 'I will choose God over humanism, our children and parents over indoctrination, and our brave law enforcement officers' (STARRS interview). Denomination not specified.
profile.background: Chandler Langevin — Brevard County native, Titusville High grad. ~10 years U.S. Navy Hospital Corpsman (FMF) attached to USMC — NATO Liaison with 4th ANGLICO in Afghanistan; sniper-platoon medic with 1st Bn 25th Marines in the Pacific. Took early honorable discharge over the Biden COVID vaccine mandate ('unwillingness to be a guinea pig'). Works in veteran advocacy; founded local youth program 'Florida Scouts'; State Director, American Restoration Coalition. Previously on Odyssey Charter School board (removed Oct 2025 over the censure controversy). Briefly filed for FL HD-33 in 2023 before withdrawing and endorsing Monique Miller. Active with Republican Liberty Caucus (Brevard chapter), Moms for Liberty (Brevard), and Space Coast Young Republicans. CENSURE / LITIGATION FACTS (recorded candidly per brand voice): On Oct 17, 2025 Palm Bay Council voted 3-2 to censure Langevin and ask Gov. DeSantis to suspend him under FS 112.51 after Langevin posted on X 'Deport every Indian immediately,' Confederate-soldier-praise posts including a description of Robert E. Lee as 'a Godly and honorable presence,' and a request on city letterhead asking President Trump to pardon white-nationalist activist Robert Rundo (founder of the Rise Above Movement). Langevin filed a federal First Amendment suit (Langevin v. Palm Bay) represented by Anthony Sabatini; bench trial began Jan 21, 2026.

profile.background: Richard 'Mike' Hammer — Palm Bay native, descendant of the founding Platt family; 22 years coaching youth football. Professional background in criminal justice and emergency medical response with civil/criminal law expertise; fire-science coursework at Indian River State College. Ran as a fiscal-restraint / 'smart growth' challenger funded by individual donors (refused developer money). Represents Palm Bay on the Brevard County Transportation Planning Organization. Voted AGAINST the Oct 17, 2025 censure of Councilman Chandler Langevin on First Amendment grounds while explicitly disagreeing with Langevin's anti-Indian-American remarks. Nonpartisan office; no primary-source party registration confirmed in this sweep.

profile.background: Michael C. 'Mike' Blake is the elected at-large Mayor of the City of Cocoa, Florida (Brevard County, pop ~17K), and a lifelong Cocoa resident. He won re-election Nov 5, 2024 defeating George Allen Cowart 62.66% (5,081) to 37.34% (3,028) for a term running through the next regular city election in 2028. His tenure on Cocoa's elected body spans 17+ years: City Council 1998-2001, Mayor 2004-2012 (two terms), District 1 Councilman 2014-June 2016, and Mayor again from 2020 to present. By profession he is a retired Cocoa High School educator (retired early 2022). Outside city hall he has chaired the Cocoa Community Redevelopment Agency and Cocoa Housing Authority, led the Space Coast League of Cities, sat on the Florida Municipal Insurance Trust Board, and rose through the Florida League of Cities (FLC) ladder: 2nd Vice President 2022-23, 1st Vice President 2023-24, and FLC President 2024-25. FLC named him a Home Rule Hero in 2023, 2025, and 2026 for advocacy during legislative sessions to push back state preemption of municipal authority. Religion / family / military / education credentials are NOT FOUND in any public source reviewed.

profile.background: Wesley 'Wes' Morrison (age ~41) is a third-generation Space Coast Floridian whose grandfather managed one of Cape Canaveral's first grocery stores in 1965. Titusville High School graduate; B.A. Political Science (Business minor), Florida State University 2004-2008 (first college graduate in his family). Re-elected November 5, 2024 with 77.09% (2,477 votes) over Angela Raymond (22.91% / 736 votes); current 3-year term runs 2024-2027. Chairs the city's Community Redevelopment Agency Board. Active Christian church-volunteer life: LifePointe Ministries (non-denominational evangelical Space Coast community church) — Vision Team Leader and Men's Group Leader since 2012; CREW29 homeless / cold-night-shelter ministry involvement. Holds an Environmental Stewardship certificate from the Institute for Humane Studies (Dec 2016). Wife Christina; daughter Parker.

Three HARD TRUE: (1) public_justice.q0 — he and wife Paula are NAMED sponsors of BCSO 'Coffee with a Cop' (per his own city bio: 'sponsoring Coffee with a Cop'), affirmatively hosted joint Council Member + BCSO LE-fraud public-safety presentation on Identity Theft/Fraud/Scams, and campaign explicitly platformed on 'Law and Order, keeping citizens safe from crime on our streets' — pattern of affirmative engagement beyond routine budget votes meets HIGH BAR; (2) refuse_state_overreach.q0 — 2025 Florida League of Cities 'Home Rule Hero' Award recipient explicitly recognized for advocacy DURING the 2025 Florida Legislative Session against state preemption of local authority; (3) refuse_state_overreach.q3 — same Home Rule Hero designation + active service on FLC Legislative Policy Committee and Federal Action Strike Team is by definition subsidiarity advocacy (lowest-competent-level decision-making vs. state/federal preemption). 27 NULL — quiet civility-coded transportation-and-budget-focused councilmember whose only RESOLUTE-visible profile is PJ.q0 + refuse_q0 + refuse_q3. profile.background: Don Willis is a retired business executive and entrepreneur with 40+ years professional experience; owned Porter Willis (technology consulting) for ~14 years serving international corporations and small businesses. BA Magna Cum Laude, University of North Carolina; Executive Education, UNC Kenan-Flagler Business School; PMP-certified. Before council, served as the city's Parks and Recreation Commissioner, Vice-Chair of the Business and Economic Development Advisory Board (4 years), and on a private school's board of directors. Married to Paula Willis; three grown children, three grandchildren. Elected to Cape Canaveral City Council November 2021 (top vote share ~25.4% of 5,174 votes), re-elected November 2023 with 38.81% (~860 votes). Currently serves as Treasurer of the Space Coast League of Cities; member of the Florida League of Cities Transportation/Intergovernmental Relations/Mobility/Emergency Management Legislative Committee, Cape Canaveral Local Roads Committee, Space Coast TPO Governing Board, FDOT District 5 Central Florida Safety Strategic Plan Steering Committee, and FLC Federal Action Strike Team. Recipient of the 2025 Florida League of Cities 'Home Rule Hero' Award for advocacy during the 2025 Legislative Session. Personally aligned with the Brevard Republican Executive Committee (party 'R' set on basis of 2021 BREC YouTube address). Religion: NOT FOUND (served on board of an unnamed private school; denomination not specified; the LinkedIn 'Don Willis - Pastor at CrossBridge Baptist' result is a DIFFERENT person, NOT this Don Willis). Part of the Davis-Willis 2-vote minority that voted to RETAIN City Manager Todd Morley in the 3-2 January 2025 termination vote.
